attendance_snippet: Staff Presentation - WMPA23-0008_WRZA23-0009 Appeal BCC 3-19-24 - Appellant Presentation - Thompson Family Trust - 3-19-24 final Attachments: Public Hearing for the second reading and possible adoption of an ordinance amending Ordinance 1615, Washoe County’s Requirements and Schedule of Rates and Charges for Sanitary Sewer Service, to amend connection fees within all service areas; and other matters properly relating thereto. The Sanitary Sewer Connection Privilege Fee Charges (Connection Charges) will increase from the current rate of $5,900.00 per equivalent residential unit (ERU) to $9,477.00 per ERU with a change to the annual rate adjustment from $50.00 per ERU to the annual averaged Engineering New Record Construction Cost Index (ENR-CCI) percentage for the previous calendar year; the Connection Fee for each Weighted Fixture Unit (WFU) will increase from the current rate of $320...

attendance_snippet: Staff Presentation - WMPA23-0008_WRZA23-0009 Appeal BCC 3-19-24 - Appellant Presentation - Thompson Family Trust - 3-19-24 final Attachments: Public Hearing for the second reading and possible adoption of an ordinance amending Ordinance 1615, Washoe County’s Requirements and Schedule of Rates and Charges for Sanitary Sewer Service, to amend connection fees within all service areas; and other matters properly relating thereto. The Sanitary Sewer Connection Privilege Fee Charges (Connection Charges) will increase from the current rate of $5,900.00 per equivalent residential unit (ERU) to $9,477.00 per ERU with a change to the annual rate adjustment from $50.00 per ERU to the annual averaged Engineering New Record Construction Cost Index (ENR-CCI) percentage for the previous calendar year; the Connection Fee for each Weighted Fixture Unit (WFU) will increase from the current rate of $320...

vote_text: . TMP-8439 Action Item Recommendation to approve the updated Washoe County Code of Conduct which lists expectations employees and Washoe County representatives in their interactions with the public and each other. The updated version reflects the current Washoe County Statement of Values. These values include integrity, effective communication, and quality public service. The Code of Conduct maintains the expectations of honesty, respect, duty to public service, tolerance, fairness, courtesy and professionalism....

TMP-8439 Action Item Recommendation to approve the updated Washoe County Code of Conduct which lists expectations employees and Washoe County representatives in their interactions with the public and each other. The updated version adds the expectation of promoting a positive work environment by prohibiting bullying, outlines the investigation process for complaints made under the Code of...

TMP-8439 Action Item Recommendation to approve the updated Washoe County Code of Conduct which lists expectations employees and Washoe County representatives in their interactions with the public and each other. The updated version reflects the current Washoe County Statement of Values. These values include integrity, effective communication, and quality public service. The Code of Conduct maintains the expectations of honesty, respect, duty to public service, tolerance, fairness, courtesy and professionalism....

attendance_snippet: Staff Presentation - WMPA23-0008_WRZA23-0009 Appeal BCC 3-19-24 - Appellant Presentation - Thompson Family Trust - 3-19-24 final Attachments: Public Hearing for the second reading and possible adoption of an ordinance amending Ordinance 1615, Washoe County’s Requirements and Schedule of Rates and Charges for Sanitary Sewer Service, to amend connection fees within all service areas; and other matters properly relating thereto. The Sanitary Sewer Connection Privilege Fee Charges (Connection Charges) will increase from the current rate of $5,900.00 per equivalent residential unit (ERU) to $9,477.00 per ERU with a change to the annual rate adjustment from $50.00 per ERU to the annual averaged Engineering New Record Construction Cost Index (ENR-CCI) percentage for the previous calendar year; the Connection Fee for each Weighted Fixture Unit (WFU) will increase from the current rate of $320...

source_snippet: . Chairman Martinez wondered what the thought was behind the reduction of the high school parking spaces. Mr. Wilkinson wanted clarification for the trash receptacle/dumpster areas. The way it's worded the developer can make the determination if they want small movable trash cans or large dumpsters. If we have an expectation that they need dumpsters, we need to be clear on that. Mr. Wilkinson wanted clarification for the trash receptacle/dumpster areas. The way it's worded the developer can make the determination if they want small movable trash cans or large dumpsters. vote_text: motion passed unanimously. public hearing. Moved by David Freistroffer, Seconded by Jose Negrete. The motion passed unanimously. Mr. Draper explained with the code changes to 3-2-17 we needed to define park and ride facilities, so we're adding that definition to 3-2-2. Additionally, we're add...
source_snippet: motion passed unanimously. public hearing. Moved by David Freistroffer, Seconded by Jose Negrete. The motion passed unanimously. Mr. Draper pointed out we do not need to modify the GI (General Industrial) code because all LI principal uses are allowed as a principal use in the GI zone. Mr. Thibault, Mr. Carson, and Mr. Wilkinson recommended approval. Mr. Draper explained with the code changes to 3-2-17 we needed to define park and ride facilities, so we're adding that definition to 3-2-2. Additionally, we're adding park and ride facilities as a principal permitted use under 3-2-12 A..

vote_outcome_snippet: motion passed unanimously. public hearing. Moved by David Freistroffer, Seconded by Jose Negrete. The motion passed unanimously. Mr. Draper pointed out we do not need to modify the GI (General Industrial) code because all LI principal uses are allowed as a principal use in the GI zone. Mr. Thibault, Mr. Carson, and Mr. Wilkinson recommended approval. Mr. Draper explained with the code changes to 3-2-17 we needed to define park and ride facilities, so we're adding that definition to 3-2-2. Additionally, we're adding park and ride facilities as a principal permitted use under 3-2-12 A..

attendance_snippet: presented and noted that he had found no statute prohibiting the approach being used. He commented that the adjustment would help address immediate recruitment concerns, though continued review might be necessary in the future. He added that the increase would move Elko County closer to the compensation levels offered in Clark County, while remaining below those rates. Chairman Andreozzi added that another screening or amendment would be appropriate so that the ordinance language would state “identified by resolution.” Mr. Ingram indicated that such a modification could be incorporated if an acceptable plan was brought forward. MOTION: Commissioner Rex Steninger moved to approve the first reading of Ordinance No. 2026-02. Commissioner Travis Gerber seconded the motion. The motion passed unanimously. -- 6 of 13 -- Elko County Board of Commissioners Meeting

source_snippet: . 4. Information Only-Student Access Funds - The Board of Regents discussed current policies that require allocating a portion of registration fees and differential fees to Student Access Funds and the policy specifying the allowable uses of those funds. The discussion included institution-specific information initially presented at the January 23, 2026, special meeting of the Board (Ref. BOR-4a and BOR-4b on file in the Board Office). Chief Financial Officer Chris Viton provided an overview of the material including the rules for fee set-asides, criteria for need-based versus non-need-based aid, and how different campuses administer these funds.
